articles.title: Nifty-gold ratio at depressed levels: What does it mean for Indian stock market investors?
articles.content: This divergence has pushed the Nifty-gold ratio to depressed levels. Gold’s roughly 52% rally against relatively muted equity returns reflects a phase of heightened defensive positioning, said Rahul Sharma of JM Financial Services.

articles.title: IREDA Q3 Results: PAT jumps 15% YoY to Rs 1,381 crore, revenue up 28%
articles.content: IREDA posted a 15.4% YoY rise in Q3 FY26 PAT at Rs 1,381 crore, with revenue up 28.2% to Rs 6,042 crore. Loan sanctions jumped 29% YoY to Rs 40,100 crore, while disbursements rose 44% to Rs 24,903 crore. Strong domestic borrowings and diversified clean energy lending underpin robust growth and execution visibility.

articles.title: Stock Market Holiday? NSE to remain open on January 15 during Maharashtra civic polls
articles.content: The Indian exchange NSE will operate for regular trading on January 15, despite Maharashtra's public holiday for civic polls. While trading will proceed, January 15 is designated a settlement holiday, meaning T+0 settlements will not be processed. Consequently, T+1 trades from both January 14 and 15 will be settled on January 16.
articles.primarySourceTitle: Stock Market Holiday? NSE to remain open on January 15 during Maharashtra civic polls

articles.title: NSE, BSE to hold mock trading session on Saturday. Can investors trade? All you need to know
articles.content: Indian stock exchanges NSE and BSE will conduct a mock trading session on Saturday, allowing brokers to test systems, risk controls and contingency mechanisms under simulated conditions. The drill includes call auctions, block deals and an outage scenario to validate Sebi’s alternate trading venue framework. No live trading will occur.
articles.primarySourceTitle: NSE, BSE to hold mock trading session on Saturday. Can investors trade? All you need to know

articles.title: Sebi eases technical glitch norms for stock brokers, cuts compliance burden for smaller firms
articles.content: SEBI revamped technical glitch norms for brokers, easing compliance by limiting applicability to firms with over 10,000 clients, simplifying reporting, granting exemptions, and rationalising penalties, offering relief to smaller brokers while maintaining technology, risk management and market integrity standards nationwide.
